title: Palhinhaea mariana
taxonomy: Palhinhaea mariana is classified in the division Lycopodiophyta, which includes the club mosses. It is further classified in the class Lycopodiopsida, order Hymnales, family Hymenophyllaceae, and genus Palhinhaea. The family Hymenophyllaceae is a group of fern-like plants, and Palhinhaea mariana is the only genus in this family. The species Palhinhaea mariana is native to the island of São Tomé in the Gulf of Guinea. It is a relatively rare and endemic species.

intro: Palhinhaea mariana is a type of fern, specifically a species of epiphytic plant that belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Palhinhaea mariana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Palhinhaea mariana is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
